gaps: 🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — No test verifies what happens when the user returns to the app AFTER the disconnect action. Test 65868088 only checks the state after unpairing, but does not specify whether the app was backgrounded/foregrounded or if the screen was refreshed. The bug is specifically about stale UI state persisting on screen return — this requires testing: (a) app was in foreground during disconnect, (b) app was backgrounded then brought back, (c) app was killed and relaunched.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER GAP — No test validates WHEN the app syncs its connection state with the Bluetooth stack. The bug implies the app may only update state on certain triggers (e.g., Bluetooth broadcast receiver, periodic poll, or only on app lifecycle events). Missing tests: (a) disconnect via Android BT settings while app is in foreground — does a BroadcastReceiver update the UI? (b) disconnect while app is backgrounded — does it sync on return? (c) disconnect then reconnect — does the app show the correct state at each transition?

🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY — The existing tests do not differentiate between device types. Poly Sync 20 has a 'Secure Connection' feature that auto-disconnects after calls — this could interact differently with the app's connection state tracking. Missing tests: (a) Sync 20 with Secure Connection enabled vs. disabled, (b) Voyager Free 60 series (multi-point earbuds) vs. single-point headsets, (c) devices with companion charge cases (Free 60, Legend 50) where the case may maintain a separate connection.

generated_testcases.title: LIFECYCLE: Disconnect via Android BT Settings while app is in foreground — verify BroadcastReceiver updates UI
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the connected device name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20+') to enter Device Details screen
Verify device status shows 'Connected'
Press Home button to go to Android Home screen (app goes to background)
Open Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Tap the gear icon next to the Poly device name
Tap 'Disconnect' (or 'Forget' if unpairing)
Observe the device status in Android BT settings shows 'Disconnected'
Return to Poly Studio Mobile app by tapping the app icon or recent apps
Observe the device status on the main screen
generated_testcases.expected: After returning to Poly Studio Mobile, the device should show as 'Disconnected' or 'Tap to connect'. The app's BroadcastReceiver should have updated the UI to reflect the Bluetooth state change. The stale 'Connected' state should NOT persist.

generated_testcases.title: LIFECYCLE: Disconnect via Android BT Settings while app is in foreground — app stays in foreground the entire time
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the connected device name to enter Device Details screen
Verify device status shows 'Connected'
Without backgrounding the app, use split-screen or quick settings to navigate to Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Tap the gear icon next to the Poly device name
Tap 'Disconnect'
Observe the device status in Android BT settings shows 'Disconnected'
Return focus to Poly Studio Mobile app (still in foreground)
Observe the device status on the main screen and Device Details screen
generated_testcases.expected: The Poly Studio Mobile app should update the device status to 'Disconnected' in real-time via the Bluetooth state BroadcastReceiver, even while the app remained in the foreground. No stale 'Connected' state should be visible.

generated_testcases.title: LIFECYCLE: Disconnect via Android BT Settings while app is backgrounded — verify state sync on return
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the connected device name to enter Device Details screen
Verify device status shows 'Connected'
Press Home button to send app to background
Open Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Tap the gear icon next to the Poly device name
Tap 'Disconnect'
Verify in Android BT settings the device shows 'Disconnected'
Wait 10 seconds
Tap the Poly Studio Mobile app icon to bring it to foreground
Observe the device status on the main screen
generated_testcases.expected: Upon returning to the foreground, the Poly Studio Mobile app should sync with the current Bluetooth stack state and display the device as 'Disconnected'. The app should not show a stale 'Connected' state.

generated_testcases.title: LIFECYCLE: Disconnect via Android BT Settings, then kill and relaunch app
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the connected device name to enter Device Details screen
Verify device status shows 'Connected'
Press Home button to send app to background
Open Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Tap the gear icon next to the Poly device name
Tap 'Disconnect'
Verify in Android BT settings the device shows 'Disconnected'
Open recent apps and swipe Poly Studio Mobile away to force-kill the app
Wait 5 seconds
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from the app launcher
Observe the device status on the main screen
generated_testcases.expected: After a fresh launch, the app should query the current Bluetooth state and display the device as 'Disconnected'. The app should not show any stale connection state from the previous session.

generated_testcases.title: STATE TRIGGER: Disconnect via Android BT Settings while on Audio Settings screen — verify state change triggers navigation
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the connected device name to enter Device Details screen
Tap 'Audio Settings' to enter the Audio Settings screen
Verify you are on the Audio Settings screen (e.g., EQ, ANC controls visible)
Without leaving the Audio Settings screen, use split-screen or quick settings to navigate to Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Tap the gear icon next to the Poly device name
Tap 'Disconnect'
Return focus to Poly Studio Mobile app — observe the Audio Settings screen
Observe the device status on the main screen after any automatic navigation
generated_testcases.expected: When the device disconnects while the user is on the Audio Settings screen, the app should either: (a) automatically navigate back to the main screen showing 'Disconnected' state, or (b) show a clear disconnected indicator/banner on the Audio Settings screen with controls disabled. The app should not remain on Audio Settings with active controls as if still connected.

generated_testcases.title: STATE TRIGGER: Disconnect → Reconnect cycle — verify app state at each transition
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app with device connected
Verify device status shows 'Connected'
Open Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Disconnect the Poly device
Return to Poly Studio Mobile app — verify status shows 'Disconnected'
Open Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Tap the Poly device name to reconnect
Verify Android BT settings shows 'Connected'
Return to Poly Studio Mobile app
Observe the device status on the main screen
generated_testcases.expected: The app should correctly reflect both transitions: 'Connected' → 'Disconnected' after the disconnect action, and 'Disconnected' → 'Connected' after the reconnect action. The app should not get stuck showing a stale state at any point in the cycle.

generated_testcases.title: DEVICE ASYMMETRY: Poly Sync 20 with Secure Connection enabled — disconnect after mobile call while app is in foreground
steps: Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20 to the test phone
Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the Sync 20 device name to enter Device Details screen
Navigate to Settings → Secure Connection and verify it is enabled (ON)
Return to the main screen — verify device shows 'Connected'
Place an outgoing mobile call from the phone (not VoIP)
During the call, observe the Poly Studio Mobile app screen
End the call
Observe the device status in Poly Studio Mobile app immediately after call ends
Wait 5 seconds and observe again
generated_testcases.expected: With Secure Connection enabled, after the call ends the Sync 20 should automatically disconnect from the phone. The Poly Studio Mobile app should update to show 'Disconnected' state within a few seconds of the call ending. The app should not remain showing 'Connected'.

generated_testcases.title: DEVICE ASYMMETRY: Poly Sync 20 with Secure Connection disabled — disconnect after mobile call while app is in foreground
steps: Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20 to the test phone
Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the Sync 20 device name to enter Device Details screen
Navigate to Settings → Secure Connection and verify it is disabled (OFF)
Return to the main screen — verify device shows 'Connected'
Place an outgoing mobile call from the phone (not VoIP)
During the call, observe the Poly Studio Mobile app screen
End the call
Observe the device status in Poly Studio Mobile app immediately after call ends
generated_testcases.expected: With Secure Connection disabled, after the call ends the Sync 20 should remain connected to the phone. The Poly Studio Mobile app should continue to show 'Connected' state. This confirms the behavior is different from the Secure Connection enabled case.

generated_testcases.title: DEVICE ASYMMETRY: Voyager Free 60 — disconnect via case closure while app is in foreground
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Free 60 earbuds to the test phone
Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the Free 60 device name to enter Device Details screen
Verify device status shows 'Connected' and earbud battery levels are visible
Place both earbuds into the charging case
Close the charging case lid
Observe the Poly Studio Mobile app screen immediately
Wait 10 seconds and observe again
Open the charging case lid and remove earbuds
Observe the app screen after earbuds reconnect
generated_testcases.expected: When the Free 60 earbuds are placed in the case and the lid is closed, the app should update to show 'Disconnected' (or 'In Case') state. When the lid is opened and earbuds are removed, the app should update back to 'Connected'. The app should correctly track the connection state through the case interaction lifecycle.

generated_testcases.title: DEVICE ASYMMETRY: Voyager Legend 50 — disconnect via case while app is backgrounded, verify on return
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Legend 50 headset to the test phone
Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Verify device status shows 'Connected'
Press Home button to send app to background
Place the Legend 50 headset into its charging case and close the lid
Wait 15 seconds for the headset to fully disconnect
Open Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth — verify device shows 'Disconnected'
Tap the Poly Studio Mobile app icon to bring it to foreground
Observe the device status on the main screen
generated_testcases.expected: Upon returning to the foreground, the Poly Studio Mobile app should reflect the current Bluetooth state and show the Legend 50 as 'Disconnected'. The app should not show a stale 'Connected' state from before the headset was placed in the case.

generated_testcases.title: STATE TRIGGER: Disconnect via Android BT Settings while app is on EQ screen — verify controls become disabled
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app with device connected
Tap the connected device name → Audio Settings → EQ
Verify EQ sliders are interactive and adjustable
Without leaving the EQ screen, use quick settings to navigate to Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Disconnect the Poly device
Return focus to Poly Studio Mobile app — observe the EQ screen
Attempt to drag any EQ slider
Navigate back to the main screen and observe device status
generated_testcases.expected: After disconnection, the EQ sliders should become disabled/greyed out and non-interactive. The app should either show a 'Disconnected' banner on the Audio Settings screen or automatically navigate back to the main screen showing 'Disconnected'. Tapping disabled sliders should produce no change.

generated_testcases.title: LIFECYCLE: Disconnect via Android BT Settings while app is in foreground — rapid disconnect/reconnect multiple times
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app with device connected
Verify device status shows 'Connected'
Open Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Disconnect the Poly device
Return to Poly Studio Mobile app — verify 'Disconnected'
Open Android Settings → Connected devices → Bluetooth
Reconnect the Poly device
Return to Poly Studio Mobile app — verify 'Connected'
Repeat steps 3-8 two more times (total of 3 disconnect/reconnect cycles)
After the final reconnect, observe the device status and any Audio Settings values
generated_testcases.expected: The app should correctly reflect the connection state after each disconnect and reconnect in the cycle. After the final reconnect, the device should show 'Connected' and any previously configured Audio Settings (EQ, ANC, Transparency) should retain their values. No stale state or data loss should occur.
